target.title: Add marketplace feed entries command
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## SummaryThis is PR8 in the hosted OpenClaw marketplace feed stack. It adds the first explicit read path over the configured hosted marketplace feed: `openclaw plugins marketplace entries`.The command lists normalized entries from the configured/default OpenClaw marketplace feed and reports whether the data came from a fresh hosted payload, an accepted hosted snapshot, or bundled fallback data. It also supports `--offline` so maintainers can prove the refreshed snapshot path without fetching the live feed.## Stack context- RFC: openclaw/rfcs#19 defines the hosted marketplace feed direction.- PR1: shape the bundled external plugin catalog as feed-like data.- PR2: lazy hosted feed fetch from the configured public marketplace feed.- PR3: hosted snapshot fallback.- PR4: persistent snapshot storage in OpenClaw state.- PR5: source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6: configurable marketplace feed/source profiles.- PR7: `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` manual snapshot refresh.- PR8: this PR, `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` manual feed/snapshot listing.## What changed- Adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` with `--feed-profile`, `--feed-url`, `--offline`, and `--json`.- Adds an explicit `offline` mode to the hosted catalog loader so callers can read the latest accepted snapshot without doing a network request.- Normalizes listed entries into id, label, kind, package/version, and install metadata.- Updates the plugin CLI docs to include `marketplace entries` and `marketplace refresh`.- Adds focused CLI and hosted catalog tests for parser dispatch, JSON output, bundled fallback output, and offline snapshot reuse.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt docs/cli/plugins.md src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `timeout 300s pnpm tsgo:core:test`- `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --base origin/feeds-hosted-catalog-marketplace-refresh`## Review noteThe final Codex review reported no discrete correctness issues. Its internal sandbox could not run tests because the read-only review sandbox could not create the heavy-check lock directory, but the local targeted tests above passed.  ## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`openclaw plugins marketplace entries --offline` reads the persisted hosted marketplace snapshot and lists normalized entries without making a fresh hosted request.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr3` at pushed PR8 head `5bcb4a40bb`, source CLI runtime harness via `node --import tsx`, same state dir previously refreshed by PR7.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Ran PR7 refresh against `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins` in `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\state-pr7-refresh`.
2. Switched to PR8 head.
3. Set the same runtime marketplace config snapshot.
4. Ran `runPluginMarketplaceEntriesCommand({ feedProfile: "clawhub", offline: true, json: true })`.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "source": "hosted-snapshot",
  "entryCount": 59,
  "metadata": { "url": "https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins", "status": 200, "checksum": "sha256:90a122c19017ef2c7a0607c8c1cb893ae7871537fe649d39080a52547bed0a7f" },
  "feed": { "id": "clawhub-official", "generatedAt": "2026-06-25T01:19:39.629Z", "sequence": 11 },
  "snapshot": { "savedAt": "2026-06-26T05:30:53.509Z" },
  "error": "hosted catalog feed offline mode",
  "firstEntry": { "label": "Expedia Travel", "id": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw", "version": "1.0.4", "install": { "clawhubSpec": "clawhub:@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw@1.0.4", "npmSpec": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw@1.0.4" } }
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The entries command returned `source: hosted-snapshot`, listed 59 entries from the persisted snapshot, preserved feed metadata, and normalized install specs for display/read paths.

What was not tested:
This proof intentionally uses offline snapshot mode, not a fresh hosted request. Fresh hosted refresh is covered by PR7.

target.title: Add marketplace feed refresh command
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## SummaryAdds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh`, a small manual activation slice for the hosted marketplace feed stack. The command reads `config.marketplaces`, calls the configured hosted external plugin catalog loader, and reports whether the accepted catalog came from the hosted feed, the last-known-good snapshot, or bundled fallback.This gives Vince and maintainers a safe way to exercise the live `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins` path and any locally configured `marketplaces.feeds` profile without changing startup, onboarding, doctor, install, or provider-repair behavior yet.## What changed- Adds `plugins marketplace refresh` with:  - `--feed-profile <name>` for a configured marketplace feed profile.  - `--feed-url <url>` for the existing guarded explicit hosted URL path.  - `--expected-sha256 <hash>` for checksum-pinned refresh checks.  - `--json` for automation-friendly source/feed/metadata output.- Keeps the command lazy-loaded with the existing plugin runtime boundary.- Reports hosted, hosted-snapshot, and bundled-fallback results.- Fails checksum-pinned refreshes when the accepted result is not a fresh hosted payload, so automation cannot silently accept bundled fallback after a checksum mismatch.- Adds focused tests for CLI routing, refresh output, fallback reporting, and pinned-refresh failure.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `timeout 300s pnpm tsgo:core:test`- `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --base origin/feeds-hosted-catalog-config-profiles` initially found the pinned-refresh fallback exit issue; fixed in this PR and reran the same review clean: no actionable regressions found.  ## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` is the first manual activation path for hosted marketplace feeds and accepts a fresh hosted payload while writing the accepted snapshot.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr3` at pushed PR7 head `b93aa0e04f`, source CLI runtime harness via `node --import tsx`, clean state dir `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\state-pr7-refresh`, live HTTPS request to `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Set runtime config snapshot with `marketplaces.feeds.clawhub.url = https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins` and source profiles for `clawhub` and `npm`.
2. Ran `runPluginMarketplaceRefreshCommand({ feedProfile: "clawhub", json: true })`.
3. Reused the same `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` in PR8 offline mode to prove the refresh wrote a durable snapshot.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "source": "hosted",
  "entries": 59,
  "metadata": { "url": "https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins", "status": 200, "checksum": "sha256:90a122c19017ef2c7a0607c8c1cb893ae7871537fe649d39080a52547bed0a7f" },
  "feed": { "id": "clawhub-official", "generatedAt": "2026-06-25T01:19:39.629Z", "sequence": 11 }
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The refresh command accepted the live hosted ClawHub payload, reported `source: hosted`, returned 59 entries, and persisted a snapshot that PR8 could read offline from the same state directory.

What was not tested:
This proof does not install plugins from the refreshed feed. Installation is a later slice.

target.title: Add hosted catalog config profiles
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## SummaryThis is PR6 in the hosted feeds stack. It wires the local OpenClaw config surface for marketplace feed profiles and local package source profiles, then adds a small bridge so the hosted external plugin catalog loader can consume `config.marketplaces` instead of taking only ad hoc in-memory profile config.This sits on PR5's live ClawHub defaults: `clawhub-public` points at `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`, `public-clawhub` represents ClawHub candidates, and `public-npm` remains available for npm-backed candidates. The config schema examples use that shape so reviewers can see how tenant or organization marketplace profiles would extend the same model. Source profiles intentionally carry only a local source name and type in this slice; registry/base URL endpoints are rejected until installer resolution can enforce them.This update also addresses Patrick's naming/API-shape feedback by using marketplace terminology for the persisted config surface: `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources`, rather than `catalog.feeds` and `catalog.sources`.This intentionally does not implement product activation of configured feeds, feed refresh scheduling, feed auth execution, signed feed verification, or installer registry switching. Auth and signed verification are rejected by this config schema until the corresponding loader/envelope enforcement exists. The purpose of this PR is to make the RFC's local ownership model concrete: feeds and sources are local config, while remote feed entries can only refer to configured source names.## What changed- Adds `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` config types and Zod schema validation.- Supports the concrete local fields used by this stack today: feed URL, refresh preferences, unsigned verification opt-in, and npm/ClawHub/Git source profile names/types.- Rejects feed auth, signed verification config, and source endpoint fields for now so users cannot configure security or installer contracts the loader does not yet enforce.- Adds config labels/help so the new section appears coherently in generated schema/docs surfaces.- Adds `loadConfiguredHostedOfficialExternalPluginCatalogEntries(...)` as the bridge from OpenClaw config to the hosted catalog loader introduced by the earlier stack.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/types.marketplaces.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/types.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/types.marketplaces.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/types.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts`- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`  ## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
OpenClaw config can provide a named hosted marketplace feed profile and local source profiles, and the hosted loader uses that profile to load the live ClawHub feed.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr3` at pushed PR6 head `8b6fa2f51aa4`, source harness via `node --import tsx`, live HTTPS request to `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Set catalog config with `feeds.clawhub.url = https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.
2. Set source profiles `clawhub.type = clawhub` and `npm.type = npm`.
3. Ran `loadHostedOfficialExternalPluginCatalogEntries({ feedProfile: "clawhub", catalogConfig, snapshotStore: null })`.
4. Final stack validation passed formatter, lint, `git diff --check`, and focused feed/marketplace/config tests.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "head": "8b6fa2f51aa4",
  "resultSource": "hosted",
  "metadata": { "url": "https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins", "status": 200, "checksum": "sha256:90a122c19017ef2c7a0607c8c1cb893ae7871537fe649d39080a52547bed0a7f" },
  "entryCount": 59,
  "feed": { "id": "clawhub-official", "sequence": 11 },
  "firstEntries": [
    { "id": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw", "npmSpec": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw@1.0.4", "clawhubSpec": "clawhub:@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw@1.0.4" }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The configured feed profile loaded the live hosted feed as `hosted`, validated configured source refs, and produced 59 entries with resolved ClawHub and npm install specs.

What was not tested:
Signed-feed enforcement and authenticated private feed profiles are intentionally later slices.

target.title: Add hosted catalog snapshot fallback
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## Journey ContextThis is step 3 of the hosted OpenClaw feed path from RFC 19. The stack is [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846) -> [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868) -> [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877). #95846 made the bundled catalog feed-shaped. #95868 added guarded hosted fetch. This PR adds the last-known-good snapshot contract that makes the hosted path safe when the register endpoint is unchanged or temporarily unhealthy.## What This PR Does- Adds a narrow `HostedOfficialExternalPluginCatalogSnapshotStore` interface.- Adds an in-memory snapshot store for tests and future runtime binding.- Writes the exact hosted response body plus hosted metadata and `savedAt` after a successful hosted load.- Uses a cached snapshot on 304, non-OK hosted responses, invalid hosted JSON/schema, or fetch failures.- Validates cached snapshot body checksum before use.- Validates cached snapshot schema before use.- Preserves `expectedSha256` pinning for snapshots, so a stale cached body cannot satisfy a caller-pinned checksum it does not match.- Falls back to the bundled catalog when no valid snapshot exists.## What This PR Intentionally Does Not DoThis PR does not choose a production persistence location, wire SQLite/runtime storage, enable the hosted loader by default, add source profiles, add auth, add signed envelopes, add regional feeds, or change search/install behavior. It defines the loader-level snapshot semantics that a later runtime-owned persistent store can bind to.## How To ReviewReview this as fallback semantics, not as the final storage implementation. The key behavior is: hosted feed first, validated last-known-good snapshot second, bundled catalog last. A snapshot write failure is intentionally non-fatal because a good hosted response should still be usable even if cache persistence fails.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` (27 tests)- `timeout 420s pnpm tsgo:core:test`- `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --uncommitted` initially found a real snapshot checksum-pinning issue; this PR fixes it. A later top-stack `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --base refs/remotes/origin/main` found the hosted-fetch lazy import issue in #95868; that is also fixed and covered. Final top-stack review reported no discrete actionable correctness issues.  ## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
The hosted loader reuses a last-known-good hosted snapshot when a later hosted fetch cannot provide an acceptable fresh payload.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr3` rebased through pushed PR3 head `be8678a805`, source test harness with in-memory snapshot store and mocked hosted responses.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts -t "uses the last known good snapshot"`
2. Final rebased-stack validation: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/marketplace.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 26 skipped (27)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

Observed result after fix:
The focused regression passed: after seeding a valid hosted snapshot, the loader returned the snapshot path instead of falling back to the bundled catalog when the fresh hosted response failed.

What was not tested:
This proof uses a mocked fetch path for deterministic snapshot failure behavior. Persistent SQLite snapshot storage is covered by the next PR.

target.title: Add hosted external catalog feed loader
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## Journey ContextThis is step 2 of the hosted OpenClaw feed path from RFC 19. The stack is [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846) -> [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868) -> [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877). #95846 made the bundled catalog feed-shaped. This PR adds the guarded hosted fetch path for that same marketplace feed, while leaving the existing synchronous bundled-catalog startup path unchanged.## What This PR Does- Adds an async hosted loader for `https://register.openclaw.ai/official-marketplace.json`.- Routes hosted fetches through the existing SSRF guard with HTTPS required and the register host allowlisted.- Sends optional `If-None-Match` and `If-Modified-Since` headers so callers can perform freshness checks.- Reads response bodies with byte and chunk-timeout limits.- Keeps the network guard import lazy so bundled-catalog cold paths do not load the guarded network stack.- Computes `sha256` metadata for the hosted response body.- Validates the hosted document against the feed-shaped official external plugin catalog schema.- Falls back to the bundled catalog on network, status, checksum, size, JSON, or schema failures.## What This PR Intentionally Does Not DoThis PR does not wire the hosted loader into startup, add persistent snapshots, add source-profile selection, add auth, add signed envelopes, add regional feeds, or change plugin install/search behavior. #95877 adds the loader-level last-known-good snapshot contract on top of this fetch path.## How To ReviewReview this as the hosted transport and validation layer. The important contract is: hosted marketplace feed can be tried safely, but any unsafe or unusable hosted result falls back to the bundled catalog instead of changing current behavior.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` (22 tests)- `timeout 300s pnpm tsgo:core:test`  ## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
The hosted catalog fetch path uses the live ClawHub plugin feed by default, accepts the live schema v2 wrapper, normalizes marketplace install candidates into current catalog metadata, and still falls back safely on invalid hosted responses.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr2` at pushed head `2d68751c140f`, source harness via `node --import tsx`, live HTTPS request to `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
2. `.\node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
3. `.\node_modules\.bin\oxlint.cmd --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
4. `git diff --check`
5. `node --import tsx -e "... loadHostedOfficialExternalPluginCatalogEntries({ snapshotStore: null }) ..."`

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "head": "2d68751c140f",
  "defaultFeedUrl": "https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins",
  "resultSource": "hosted",
  "metadata": { "url": "https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins", "status": 200, "checksum": "sha256:90a122c19017ef2c7a0607c8c1cb893ae7871537fe649d39080a52547bed0a7f" },
  "entryCount": 59,
  "feed": { "id": "clawhub-official", "sequence": 11 },
  "firstEntries": [
    { "name": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw", "id": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw", "label": "Expedia Travel", "npmSpec": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw@1.0.4", "integrity": "sha256:b355dda04403becaab8bbab069fd1e7b0578262e7459e598cc5b19615b5bdab9" }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The default hosted fetch accepted the live ClawHub feed as `hosted`, returned 59 installable entries, preserved checksum/validators, and normalized a marketplace candidate into `npmSpec` plus integrity metadata.

What was not tested:
Snapshot reuse is not part of this PR. It is covered by the next snapshot-cache PR.

target.title: Refactor external plugin catalog toward feeds
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## Journey ContextThis is step 1 of the hosted OpenClaw feed path from RFC 19. The stack is [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846) -> [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868) -> [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877). This first PR does the smallest safe prep: it makes the bundled official external plugin catalog look like the feed document we want to host later, while preserving the current local behavior.## What This PR Does- Adds feed metadata around `scripts/lib/official-external-plugin-catalog.json` without changing the existing `entries` payload.- Adds parser/type support for the feed-shaped bundled catalog.- Preserves legacy local catalog parsing for existing unversioned catalog shapes.- Fails closed for unsupported versioned feed wrappers so future schema versions do not silently parse as legacy catalogs.## What This PR Intentionally Does Not DoThis PR does not fetch from `register.openclaw.ai`, add auth, add source profiles, add signed envelopes, add regional selection, add snapshot persistence, change search behavior, or move skills into feeds. Those are separate reviewable slices.## How To ReviewReview this as a mechanical shape change plus compatibility guard. The expected behavior is that existing callers still get the same official external plugin entries from the bundled catalog, but the bundled file is now ready to serve as the local fallback for a hosted feed.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- JSON parse of `scripts/lib/official-external-plugin-catalog.json`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` (17 tests at initial PR authoring)  ## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
The feed-shaped bundled fallback is still loaded by existing runtime catalog consumers after the catalog wrapper refactor.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr1` at `44c0884c3edc`, source harness via `node --import tsx`, no network services.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Imported `src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts`.
2. Imported `src/plugins/web-search-install-catalog.ts`.
3. Listed official external catalog entries and web-search consumer candidates from the bundled fallback.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "head": "44c0884c3edc",
  "bundledFeedAccepted": true,
  "officialEntryCount": 74,
  "firstOfficialIds": ["wecom-openclaw-plugin", "openclaw-plugin-yuanbao", "openclaw-weixin", "openclaw-zaloclawbot"],
  "webSearchConsumerCount": 9,
  "webSearchConsumerSample": [
    { "pluginId": "brave", "provider": "brave", "npmSpec": "@openclaw/brave-plugin" },
    { "pluginId": "exa", "provider": "exa", "npmSpec": "@openclaw/exa-plugin" },
    { "pluginId": "firecrawl", "provider": "firecrawl", "npmSpec": "@openclaw/firecrawl-plugin" }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The bundled feed wrapper validates, the existing official catalog returns 74 entries, and a real catalog consumer still resolves web-search install candidates from that feed-shaped fallback.

What was not tested:
Hosted network fetching is intentionally not part of this PR. It is covered by the next hosted-fetch PR.

target.title: Add hosted catalog source profile validation
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## StackStep 5 in the hosted feed rollout. This is stacked on `feeds-hosted-catalog-persistent-snapshot` after:- #95846 reshapes the bundled external plugin catalog as a feed-shaped fallback- #95868 adds the guarded hosted feed loader- #95877 adds the last-known-good snapshot fallback contract- #95964 persists hosted catalog snapshots in OpenClaw state## RFC alignmentThis PR adds the first local feed/source profile scaffolding from RFC #19. The important boundary is that profile names are local OpenClaw configuration. A hosted feed can refer to a configured `sourceRef`, but it cannot define registry domains, credentials, or trust roots for the client.This now matches the live ClawHub plugin feed at `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`, which uses `public-clawhub` candidates. The PR also keeps the older register host allowed for transition, while `registry.openclaw.ai` is still being brought online.## Summary- Adds local profile types for hosted catalog feeds and package sources- Adds the default `clawhub-public` feed profile pointing at `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`- Adds the default `public-clawhub` source profile for ClawHub candidates and keeps `public-npm` for npm-backed candidates- Lets named local feed profiles authorize their configured HTTPS host through the existing SSRF guard path- Keeps direct ad hoc `feedUrl` overrides constrained to the public host allowlist, including `clawhub.ai` and the earlier register host- Filters feed entries that opt into `install.candidates[].sourceRef` when the source ref is missing or not configured locally- Applies the same source-ref filtering to hosted responses and hosted snapshot fallback- Recognizes RFC-style `id` and `title` fields for catalog identity and labels so live ClawHub entries are not dropped before the later install-mapping slice## Deliberately not included- Feed/source profile persistence in user config files- Secret-reference resolution or authenticated feed/source access- Signed feed envelopes or trust-key configuration- Scheduled refresh, `maxStale`, or stale-catalog status reporting- Changing plugin install execution to consume candidate source profiles or translating RFC candidates into installer specs- Search, install UX, skills, or telemetry## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
This PR validates hosted marketplace install source refs against local source profiles and accepts the live ClawHub plugin feed format now served as `schemaVersion: 2`.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04, `/root/src/openclaw-feeds-hosted-catalog-prep`, PR5 and cumulative hosted marketplace feed stack through PR8 at `13cea8e00e7`, built source tree with `pnpm build`, isolated OpenClaw state via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX`, and the live ClawHub feed endpoint `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. On PR5: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts --reporter=dot`
2. On PR8 after rebasing PR6-PR8: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/marketplace.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts --reporter=dot`
3. `curl -sS -D - https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins -o /tmp/clawhub-plugins.json`
4. `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX node openclaw.mjs plugins marketplace entries --feed-url https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`

Evidence after fix:
PR5 focused catalog tests passed: `1 passed / 38 tests`. Full stack focused tests passed across 3 Vitest shards: `1 passed / 8 tests`, `2 passed / 7 tests`, and `2 passed / 70 tests`. Live curl showed `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`, `X-Content-Sha256: 99b2608a539fe54d0d0ded2182574b62b8b772bd2011d9eb497147be23ff2c2e`, `Last-Modified: Wed, 24 Jun 2026 13:06:20 GMT`, and a body beginning with `{ "schemaVersion": 2, "id": "clawhub-official" ... }`. The built CLI returned `Source: hosted`, `Entries: 59`, `Feed: clawhub-official sequence 9`, and live ClawHub plugin entries.

Observed result after fix:
The live ClawHub v2 feed is accepted, entries with `public-clawhub` source refs resolve to `clawhub:` and npm install choices, and unsupported future schema versions still stay out of legacy parsing in tests.

What was not tested:
No live install was run. This PR validates and lists feed metadata only; install wiring remains a later slice.

target.title: Doctor: add lint --all
target.body:
What Problem This Solves
- Doctor now has warning-level checks that are intentionally excluded from the default `doctor --lint` automation gate because they are deeper or more likely to surface repairable legacy residue.
- Before this PR, those checks were discoverable only by exact `--only <check-id>` selectors, which is too hidden for operators who want the complete lint inventory.

Why This Change Was Made
- Adds `openclaw doctor --lint --all`.
- Keeps plain `doctor --lint` as the existing automation-safe selection.
- Lets `--all` include default-disabled checks without changing finding severity, finding schemas, repair behavior, config, or the plugin SDK health contract.
- Leaves `--only <id>` as the precise selector for individual checks.
- Keeps `--all` lint-only; `openclaw doctor --all` is rejected like other lint selector flags.

User Impact
- Existing `doctor --lint` users keep the same broad-lint behavior.
- Operators and maintainers can run `doctor --lint --all` to include opt-in/deep checks such as the config audit scrub check from #84450.
- This is a CLI/docs contract addition only; no config, plugin, or SDK contract expansion.

Evidence
- Rebased after #95976 merged; current PR diff is only the `doctor --lint --all` slice.
- Current head `560d59f2f5e18a32d97bca672aa8536f8cc6c045`.
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts --reporter=dot` - passed, 32 tests / 3 shards.
- `pnpm docs:list` - passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts docs/cli/doctor.md docs/gateway/doctor.md` - passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts` - passed.
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1` - passed.
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1` - passed.
- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check` - passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- Hosted exact-head CI/Testbox gates passed on `560d59f2f5e18a32d97bca672aa8536f8cc6c045`.

Real Behavior Proof
- Environment: source CLI from this branch, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, with one seeded `logs/config-audit.jsonl` record containing a fake pre-redactor argv value. Paths below are redacted.

```text
--- default lint ---
command: node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json
exit: 1
checksRun: 25
checksSkipped: 2
configAuditFindingCount: 0
--- all lint ---
command: node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --all
exit: 1
checksRun: 27
checksSkipped: 0
configAuditFindingCount: 1
configAuditFinding: warning core/doctor/config-audit-scrub 1 entry in config-audit.jsonl still contain pre-redactor argv values. path=<TEMP>\state\logs\config-audit.jsonl
```

- The non-zero exit in both runs is expected because the isolated minimal config also produces unrelated default-lint warnings. The proof target is the selection behavior: default lint skips the opt-in config-audit check, while `--all` includes it.

target.title: fix(mcp): reject mixed command+url transport config (#72111)
target.body:
Closes #72111.

## Problem

When an MCP server config carries **both** a non-empty `command` and a non-empty `url` (e.g. a streamable-http endpoint plus a `command`/`args` pair), the runtime silently picks stdio and drops the HTTP transport. The user sees:

```
MCP server connection timed out after 30000ms
```

The spawned child never speaks JSON-RPC over stdio because the user actually wanted the streamable-http endpoint. The clawsweeper bot triaged this and recommended early rejection over silent fallback.

## Root cause

`resolveMcpTransportConfig` in `src/agents/mcp-transport-config.ts` resolves the stdio launch first when `command` is set, regardless of whether `url` is also set. The 30s timeout that follows is the user-visible symptom; the silent fallback at config-resolution time is the real bug.

## Reproducer

In `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`:

```json
{
  \"mcp\": {
    \"servers\": {
      \"broken\": {
        \"command\": \"node\",
        \"args\": [\"something.js\"],
        \"url\": \"https://my-mcp.example.com/mcp\"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Run OpenClaw → the `broken` server hangs, then errors with `MCP server connection timed out after 30000ms`. There is no clue in the logs about *which* transport was actually attempted.

## Fix

Reject the ambiguous shape early with a clear diagnostic that names the offending server and the requested transport hint, matching the file's existing \"skipped server X because Y\" diagnostic style and the clawsweeper bot's \"Best possible solution\":

> reject mixed `command` + `url`/HTTP transport configs early with a clear diagnostic and docs/test alignment. The current silent stdio precedence should not remain the user-visible timeout path.

Implementation: when `command` and a non-whitespace `url` are both present, log the warning, return `null` (caller already handles `null` as \"skip this server\"), and let the user fix their config.

Pure stdio configs (no `url`) and pure HTTP configs (no `command`) are unaffected. **Whitespace-only `url`** strings continue to be treated as \"no url\" so stdio still wins, matching the existing trimming in `mcp-stdio.ts`.

Diff: **+17** lines in `mcp-transport-config.ts`, **+52** in tests.

## Verification

```
pnpm vitest run src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts
# 11 passed (7 original + 4 new)

pnpm check
# 0 errors, 0 warnings; all policy guards green
```

## Tests added (4 new)

1. **Mixed config + transport hint** → resolution returns `null`; warning lists both `command` and `url` and the requested transport.
2. **Mixed config without explicit transport hint** → still rejected; warning is informative.
3. **Whitespace-only `url` paired with real `command`** → resolution returns stdio (mirrors `mcp-stdio.ts` trimming).
4. **Sanitized transport in warning** → diagnostic does not leak full URL secrets / query strings.

## Risk notes

- **Behavior change is user-visible**: configs that used to silently fall back to stdio when both `command`+`url` were set now get rejected outright (server skipped, no spawn). That is the bot-blessed shape and matches the issue, but anyone relying on the silent fallback (unlikely — it was the bug) will see a behavior shift on upgrade.
- Did NOT touch `src/agents/mcp-stdio.ts` even though the bot's evidence cites it; the rejection is cleaner at the transport-config layer where both fields are visible together.
- PR collision check: 8 open MCP-related PRs in the repo (#66542, #69417, #69039, #65574, #62134, #64316, #62160, #60005); none touch `mcp-transport-config.ts` validation logic for the mixed shape. Safe to land independently.

target.title: fix(group-chat): wire multi-agent bot-targeting signals end-to-end (#56692)
target.body:
Closes #56692.

## Problem

In a group chat with multiple bots, an agent can't tell which messages in the recent history were addressed to **itself** vs. another bot in the same group. `InboundHistory` and the reply context only carry sender / body / timestamp; the targeting signal (mention entity, reply-chain `from`) gets dropped at the channel-plugin boundary.

The result: the agent treats "@otherbot how are you" the same as a direct address, occasionally hallucinating an answer to a message that was never directed at it.

## Root cause

Three independent gaps:

1. `HistoryEntry` and the inline `InboundHistory` type in `templating.ts` have no fields to carry per-message bot-targeting metadata.
2. `MsgContext` (template context) has no `OtherBotUsernames` / `ReplyToBotUsername` to surface the multi-agent layout to the prompt.
3. The Telegram channel plugin parses mention entities and `reply_to_message.from.is_bot` in `bot-message-context.body.ts` / `bot-message-context.session.ts` but never propagates that signal to the new fields.

A prior PR (#58102, closed by author 2026-04-20, **not rejected by maintainer** — Greptile gave it 5/5 "safe to merge") proposed the core type additions but explicitly left "channel plugins need to populate" as follow-up. That follow-up never happened.

## Reproducer

1. Add two bots, `@bot-a` and `@bot-b`, to a Telegram group, both running OpenClaw.
2. Send a message: `@bot-b what's the weather?`
3. Then send a follow-up unrelated message that pings `@bot-a`: `hey @bot-a remember when @bot-b answered the weather question?`
4. Observe `bot-a`'s prompt: it sees the prior `what's the weather?` message **without any signal** that it was directed at `bot-b`. `bot-a` will sometimes answer it as if it were addressed to itself.

## Fix

End-to-end wiring of the design from #58102, plus the missing channel populate:

**Core (`src/auto-reply/`)**
- `HistoryEntry`: optional `mentionedBot` / `repliedToBot` (`string | null`). Tri-state: `undefined` = legacy/unknown caller, `null` = explicitly no bot targeted, `string` = bot username.
- `MsgContext`: optional `OtherBotUsernames: string[]`, `ReplyToBotUsername: string`. Individual entries are trimmed before render (Greptile fix #58102 missed at `groups.ts:157,210`).
- `inbound-meta.ts`: emit `mentioned_bot` / `replied_to_bot` per history entry and a top-level `replied_to_bot` for the current reply target. `null` is preserved through JSON; `undefined` is dropped to keep the payload lean.
- `groups.ts`: `buildGroupChatContext` adds bot-username + multi-agent disambiguation guidance only when `BotUsername` is set, preserving byte-stable output for callers that don't pass it. `buildGroupIntro` mirrors that.

**Telegram channel plugin (`extensions/telegram/src/`)**
- New `detectTelegramBotTargeting()` helper: entity-mention scan with plain-text fallback, plus `reply_to_message.from.is_bot` derivation.
- `TelegramReplyTarget` gains `senderIsBot`, derived from `from.is_bot`.
- `bot-message-context.body.ts`: populate `HistoryEntry` bot fields on the group-skip path so future turns see who the prior message targeted.
- `bot-message-context.session.ts`: forward bot fields through `inboundHistory` to the prompt; populate `ReplyToBotUsername` when the visible reply target is a bot.

`OtherBotUsernames` is left unset until the Telegram config exposes a sibling-bot list — the prompt-rendering code handles `undefined` gracefully.

## Backward compatibility

All new fields are optional. Output for callers that don't provide `BotUsername` or sibling-bot config is **byte-identical** to today. The 4 pre-existing `groups` tests and existing inbound-meta tests stay green unchanged.

## Verification

```
pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.test.ts
# 64 passed (21 new)

pnpm vitest run extensions/telegram
# 1447 passed across 100 files, no regressions

pnpm check
# preflight ✅, tsgo:core ✅, tsgo:extensions ✅,
# oxlint core/extensions/scripts (13000+ files): 0 warnings, 0 errors,
# all policy guards (temp-path-guardrails, no-pairing-store-group, webhook auth body order,
#   pairing-account-scope, runtime-action-load-config, import-cycles): pass
```

## Tests added

- 11 new auto-reply tests: null vs undefined semantics for `mentionedBot`/`repliedToBot` in history and reply context, `OtherBotUsernames` trim+filter, multi-agent intro generation, byte-stable backward compat for callers without `BotUsername`, empty/whitespace omission.
- 10 new Telegram body-helpers tests: entity mention parsing, plain-text mention fallback, reply-to-bot derivation from `is_bot`, no-entities edge case, non-bot sender, missing username.

## Scope honesty

- Only the Telegram channel plugin is wired in this PR. Other channel plugins (Discord, Slack, Lark, Feishu, …) can populate the same fields when their respective parsers are extended; the core contracts are stable and the prompt-rendering code already handles missing data.
- `OtherBotUsernames` populate is gated on a future "sibling-bot list" surface in Telegram config; flagged with an inline `// Multi-agent (#56692):` note at the population site.

target.title: perf(qmd): persistent export-state cache + stat fast path in exportSessions
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** For agents with thousands of session transcripts, `QmdMemoryManager.exportSessions()` was the dominant gateway CPU consumer. Every boot and every QMD sync interval walked every session JSONL and called `buildSessionEntry()` per file, which performs full read + parse + redaction + hash/render work even when the transcript export was unchanged.
- **Why it matters:** The previous in-memory skip state only avoided some markdown rewrites after a manager instance had already rebuilt each entry. It did not survive gateway restart, so cold starts and periodic syncs could still create large CPU and GC spikes on production-sized transcript sets.
- **What changed:**
  1. Added a per-agent SQLite cache table, `qmd_session_export_cache`, in `openclaw-agent.sqlite`, keyed by `(session_file, export_dir, render_version)`.
  2. Added a stat + full-content-fingerprint fast path before `buildSessionEntry()`. When cached `size`, `mtimeMs`, `ino`, full content fingerprint, and cached markdown target all match, QMD skips the expensive full-session rebuild.
  3. Preserved the slow path for changed transcripts, missing markdown targets, mismatched cached targets, retention pruning, export-dir changes, and render-version changes.
  4. Kept raw per-agent DB/Kysely access inside the core state owner and exposed only QMD-specific cache helpers through a private local-only SDK subpath, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/memory-core-qmd-export-cache-internal`, scoped to the bundled `memory-core` plugin.
  5. Batch-loaded the current export scope cache rows once per `exportSessions()` pass and reused an in-memory lookup map, so large transcript sets do not reopen/refresh the per-agent DB accessor once per session file.
  6. Excluded the generated private QMD SDK helper artifacts from npm package output: `!dist/plugin-sdk/memory-core-qmd-export-cache-internal.js` and `!dist/plugin-sdk/memory-core-qmd-export-cache-internal.d.ts`.

**Scope boundary:** `renderSessionMarkdown()`, session redaction, line wrapping, and the markdown format QMD consumes are unchanged. `buildSessionEntry()` remains the authoritative slow path. The cache is scoped by export directory and `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION`, so config moves and render-byte changes invalidate cleanly.

**Effect on Eddie's main agent (4,052 sessions / 158 MB) — prior-head context, not current-head proof:**
- Boot QMD sync: **312s -> single-digit seconds**
- Interval QMD sync: **38s -> ~50-500 ms**
- The every-5-min CPU spike + GC tail collapses

> ⚠️ **Benchmark provenance:** these numbers were measured on an early head of
> this branch and were **not** re-run after the SQLite-backed cache rewrite,
> the private-SDK boundary work, the full-content-hash fast path, the
> target-byte fingerprint validation, or the latest `origin/main` merge. They
> remain a faithful description of the *mechanism's* impact (eliminating the
> per-file `buildSessionEntry()` rebuild on unchanged transcripts) but should
> be treated as **observational context, not current-head benchmark proof**.
> Re-running them requires Eddie's live 4,052-session production corpus on the
> running gateway, which is not reproducible from an agent worktree. The
> `## Real Behavior Proof` section below is the authoritative current-head
> behavioral evidence.

## Linked Issue

- Closes openclaw-ys0 (Beads tracking issue)

## Test Coverage

New and updated tests cover the cache contract:

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
  - persists session-export state across manager restarts
  - rebuilds after same-size transcript rewrites, proving content changes are not hidden by matching stat fields
  - ignores cache rows scoped to a previous `exportDir`
  - rebuilds markdown when the cached target has been deleted
  - rebuilds the expected markdown when a current-scope cache row points at another target
  - preserves existing session-export behavior for checkpoint/session filtering
- `src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts`
  - schema shape includes the new derived-state cache table
  - per-agent DB schema metadata and `user_version` remain at version 1
  - newer-schema refusal still protects downgrade safety
- `test/release-check.test.ts`
  - package output rejects private local-only plugin SDK artifacts
  - package `files` exclusions include every private local-only SDK artifact, including the new QMD cache helper

## Tradeoffs / Known Caveats

- The fast path still uses stat identity to avoid hashing obviously changed files, but it verifies a full SHA-1 content fingerprint before skipping the rebuild. Same-size rewrites with preserved or seeded stat identity do not suppress updated markdown.
- `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` must be bumped when changing `renderSessionMarkdown()`, redaction, line wrapping, or any other logic that changes the bytes written to `qmd/sessions/*.md`.
- The cache table is derived state; the per-agent DB `user_version` remains at 1 for rollback safety.
- No hand-written `CHANGELOG.md` entry is included; current repo policy keeps release notes out of normal PRs and derives them during release generation.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior addressed:** QMD session export persists its unchanged-transcript fast-path state in the per-agent SQLite DB without exposing raw per-agent DB/Kysely access through the public plugin SDK. It must still rebuild markdown when a cached target is missing, when the cached target points elsewhere, when stat identity matches but the transcript content fingerprint changed, **and when the export target's own bytes were externally modified/corrupted while the source transcript stayed unchanged** (target-byte fingerprint validation; self-heals on the next sync). The private QMD SDK artifact must also stay out of package output.

- **Canonical current-head proof script:** `scripts/proof-77158-qmd-export-target-validation.ts` (run with `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-77158-qmd-export-target-validation.ts`). It drives the **real private `QmdMemoryManager.exportSessions()` method** — not a re-implementation — on a real `QmdMemoryManager.create({ mode: "status" })` instance against real per-agent SQLite + the real session-transcript corpus accessor + real fs (no Vitest, no mocked cache seam). `mode: "status"` initializes collections without spawning the `qmd` binary, and `exportSessions()` shells out to nothing, so the entire seam (stat fast path, source content fingerprint, target-byte fingerprint, slow-path rebuild, cache upsert, stale-entry deletion, artifact-mapping write) runs as shipped. Scenarios: (a) cold cache → builds + persists a non-null target fingerprint (asserted == SHA-1 of the written bytes); (b) clean hit → skip with no rewrite (target mtime preserved); (c) corrupted target bytes → rebuild + self-heal (the pinned regression); (d) source changed → rebuild with new content; (e.1) legacy NULL `target_fingerprint` + correct bytes → repopulate the fingerprint, then verified skip; (e.2) legacy NULL + corrupted target → rebuild. Output ends `All runtime assertions passed.` Focused vitest regressions in `qmd-manager.test.ts` pin the same paths in the unit suite.
- **Real environment tested:** Current PR head `3c923479bc` in the `perf/qmd-export-stat-fast-path` worktree, using the real `QmdMemoryManager`, real `resolveMemoryBackendConfig`, the real `listSessionTranscriptCorpusEntriesForAgent` accessor (resolved through the real runtime-config snapshot), the private local-only QMD cache subpath, and real per-agent `openclaw-agent.sqlite` storage. The proof ran against an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/tmp`; no Vitest harness or mocked QMD export cache was used, and the `qmd` binary was never invoked (it is not on the export path).
- **Exact steps run:** `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-77158-qmd-export-target-validation.ts` from the topic worktree. The script seeds one session JSONL + a `sessions.json` identity, then calls the real `exportSessions()` repeatedly while mutating the source transcript, the on-disk markdown target, and the cache row's `target_fingerprint` to exercise each scenario; every SKIP-vs-REBUILD decision is made by the shipped method.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the live run (also saved under `~/reports/proof-77158/run-output.txt`):

```text
[a] cold export -> built target and persisted target fingerprint
[b] clean cache hit -> skipped rebuild (target untouched)
[c] corrupted target bytes -> rebuilt and self-healed (regression pinned)
[d] source changed -> rebuilt with new content
[e.1] legacy NULL fingerprint + correct bytes -> repopulated, then verified skip
[e.2] legacy NULL fingerprint + corrupted target -> rebuilt (corruption healed)
All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** (a) the cold export built the markdown and persisted a non-null target fingerprint equal to the SHA-1 of the written bytes; (b) the clean hit left the target's mtime and content untouched, proving the verified fast path; (c) a corrupted target was rebuilt and self-healed, with the next round skipping again; (d) a changed source rebuilt with the new content; (e.1) a legacy NULL-fingerprint row (correct bytes) re-entered the slow path and repopulated the fingerprint, then the next round was a verified skip; (e.2) a legacy NULL-fingerprint row over a corrupted target rebuilt rather than trusting the unverifiable bytes. Final cache state tracks exactly the one exported session.
- **What was not tested:** I did not rerun a benchmark against Eddie's full 4,052-session production corpus after the SDK-boundary rewrite, base-branch merge, package exclusion fix, cached-target validation fix, target-byte fingerprint validation, and batch-read follow-up. That corpus lives on Eddie's running gateway and is not reproducible from an agent worktree. The proof above (and `scripts/proof-77158-qmd-export-target-validation.ts`) validates the current SQLite cache behavior and all regression paths on real runtime code; the original production-sized timing numbers remain observational context, not current-head benchmark proof (see the ⚠️ caveat under Summary).

## Verification

Current head `3c923479bc` includes:

- `3c923479bc` (`perf(qmd): real exportSessions() integration proof; drop changelog edit (review feedback)`) — the latest review round: (1) the proof script now drives the **real private `exportSessions()`** method end to end (see `## Real Behavior Proof` above) instead of re-implementing the cache decision inline; (2) the release-owned `CHANGELOG.md` `## Unreleased` block is removed (restored byte-identical to `main`).
- `2d3cc42a55` (`perf(qmd): validate cached export target bytes before skip (review feedback)`) — the [P2] target-byte fingerprint validation fix: the fast path now reads the export target and compares its SHA-1 against the cached rendered-markdown fingerprint before skipping; on drift (or a legacy NULL fingerprint) it falls through to the slow path, which self-heals the corrupted target. Adds a nullable `target_fingerprint` column via additive `ALTER` (no schema-version bump).
- `33a9ac2566` (`chore(qmd): refresh plugin SDK API baseline after main merge (review feedback)`) — regenerated `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` (tracked file only; `.json`/`.jsonl` are gitignored) after the latest `origin/main` merge shifted the generated SDK surface (corpus refactor #96162 and adjacent memory-host-sdk work). Deterministic across repeated `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen` runs.
- `3468c322a6` (`fix(qmd): exclude private cache SDK artifact from package`)
- signed base-refresh merges from current `openclaw/main` to clear the PR's dirty/conflicting merge state
- cleanup restoring unrelated Slack/Telegram/installer/test formatter churn back to current `openclaw/main`, leaving exactly the QMD/cache/SDK/package diff

**Corpus-entry preservation ([P2] #2 in the latest Codex review): verified, no change needed.** Current head's `exportSessions()` already iterates `listSessionTranscriptCorpusEntriesForAgent(this.agentId)` and threads each `corpusEntry` into both `buildSessionEntry()` (`generatedByDreamingNarrative` / `generatedByCronRun`) and `buildSessionArtifactMapping()` — in the fast path AND the slow path — then calls `replaceQmdSessionArtifactMappings(...)`. The `buildSessionArtifactMapping` body is byte-identical to `origin/main`. The accessor-backed corpus identity flow from #96162 is fully preserved; the cache layer short-circuits only the expensive rebuild, never the corpus identity mapping.

**Schema-version-1 derived-cache table — maintainer acceptance item (not a code bug):** the new per-agent `qmd_session_export_cache` table (and its additive `target_fingerprint` column) intentionally keeps `OPENCLAW_AGENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1` because it is disposable derived state — a cold/older build simply rebuilds the cache; there is no migration to roll back. This is a deliberate rollback-safety decision that needs a maintainer's explicit acceptance, not a version bump. Flagged for maintainer judgment rather than changed unilaterally.

**Prior art for the version-1 additive-table strategy:** every table ever added to `openclaw-agent.sqlite` has followed the same convention — `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` at `user_version = 1`, no version bump, no migration required. The full history on `origin/main`:

| PR / commit | Tables added | Schema version after |
|---|---|---|
| `#88349` (Add per-agent SQLite cache store) | `schema_meta`, `cache_entries` | 1 |
| `#89102` (store auth profiles in sqlite) | `auth_profile_store`, `auth_profile_state` | 1 |
| memory index PRs | `memory_index_meta`, `memory_index_sources`, `memory_index_chunks`, `memory_embedding_cache`, `memory_index_state` | 1 |
| **this PR** | `qmd_session_export_cache` | **1** |

The only time a version bump was attempted (`fix/heartbeat-claude-auth-schema-v2`, a compat shim for v2-stamped agent DBs) it was reverted. The project has consistently treated additive derived-state tables as rollback-safe at version 1; a version bump is reserved for changes that require forward-only migration and must block rollback. `qmd_session_export_cache` contains no user data and can be silently orphaned by a downgrade with no behavioral consequence, which is exactly the class of change this strategy is designed for.

**Base status (GitHub `mergeable: CONFLICTING`):** the branch already contains all of the fork's `main`; the conflict is pure base drift — upstream `main` is currently a few hours / ~31 commits ahead of the fork `main` this branch is built from. A trial merge of current upstream `main` produced exactly one auto-resolvable conflict (the generated `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`), with `qmd-manager.ts`, its test, and `package.json` all auto-merging cleanly — so this is a clean base-advance that clears once the routine fork sync lands and the baseline is regenerated. No substantive code conflict exists.

Commands run after the package exclusion fix and/or after the base merge:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/release-check.test.ts
# 56 passed

node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs
# OK: all referenced openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath> imports are exported

node scripts/check-extension-plugin-sdk-boundary.mjs --mode=src-outside-plugin-sdk
# no violations; baseline matches

node scripts/check-extension-plugin-sdk-boundary.mjs --mode=plugin-sdk-internal
# no violations; baseline matches

pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
# OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check
# all SDK entrypoints: 339; public package SDK entrypoints: 321; local-only entrypoints: 18; package-exported forbidden subpaths: 0

pnpm exec oxfmt --check package.json docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-qmd-export-cache-internal.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.ts scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-private-local-only-subpaths.json
# clean

pnpm tsgo:core
# exited 0

pnpm tsgo:core:test
# exited 0

git diff --check
# clean

node --import tsx --input-type=module
# real-runtime QMD/SQLite proof above; All runtime assertions passed.
```

target.title: fix(qwen): enable qwen3.6-plus on Coding Plan CN, correct reasoning flag
target.body:
## Summary

- **What changed**: Enables `qwen3.6-plus` on the DashScope Coding Plan CN endpoint (`coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) and corrects its `reasoning` flag (`false` → `true`).
  - `extensions/qwen/models.ts`: `reasoning: false` → `reasoning: true` on the `qwen3.6-plus` catalog entry; `buildQwenModelCatalogForBaseUrl` simplified to return the full catalog for all base URLs (qwen3.6-plus no longer excluded from Coding Plan URLs).
  - `extensions/qwen/openclaw.plugin.json`: removes the two `modelCatalog.suppressions` entries that blocked `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan configurations — confirmed available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`.
  - `extensions/qwen/index.ts`: removes `normalizeConfig` (which stripped `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan `providerConfig.models`); no longer needed after manifest suppression removal.
- **Why it matters**: `qwen3.6-plus` is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` (DashScope CN Coding Plan). The existing suppression incorrectly hid it from Coding Plan users. The `reasoning: false` flag causes OpenClaw to suppress thinking-block rendering for a model that returns `reasoning_content`.
- **What did NOT change**: Auth flow, other model entries, endpoint constants, and Standard endpoint behavior are untouched. Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior is not claimed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations (Qwen / DashScope provider catalog)
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Standalone catalog update; no linked issue.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: `qwen3.6-plus` was suppressed on Coding Plan CN and showed `reasoning: false`. After this patch it is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` and the reasoning flag is accurate.
- **Environment tested**: DashScope CN Coding Plan endpoint (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`), live API key, direct HTTP.
- **Steps run after the patch**:
  1. Sent a completion request for `qwen3.6-plus` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1` with a live Coding Plan API key.
  2. Inspected response for `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirming thinking-mode support.
- **Observed result**: `qwen3.6-plus` accepted on Coding Plan CN endpoint; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` present in response, confirming `reasoning: true` is correct.
- **Evidence**:

<details>
<summary>Live response — qwen3.6-plus on coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com</summary>

```text
POST https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1/chat/completions
Authorization: Bearer sk-<redacted>
```

```json
{
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is 2+2? Answer in one word."}],
  "max_tokens": 50
}
```

```json
{
  "choices": [{
    "message": {
      "content": "Four",
      "reasoning_content": "Here's a thinking process:\n\n1. Question: \"What is 2+2?\"\n2. 2 + 2 = 4\n3. One word: Four",
      "role": "assistant"
    },
    "finish_reason": "stop",
    "index": 0
  }],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 22,
    "completion_tokens": 227,
    "total_tokens": 249,
    "completion_tokens_details": {"reasoning_tokens": 221, "text_tokens": 227}
  },
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus"
}
```

`qwen3.6-plus` accepted on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirmed.

</details>

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- Risk summary: Catalog metadata change only. Removes an incorrect suppression; no new endpoint, auth flow, or runtime surface introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Endpoint: DashScope CN Coding Plan (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`)
- Auth: DashScope Coding Plan API key (`sk-<redacted>`)

### Steps

1. POST completion request with `"model": "qwen3.6-plus"` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`.
2. Inspect response for `reasoning_content` and `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`.

### Expected

`qwen3.6-plus` responds on Coding Plan CN with `reasoning_content` present.

### Actual

Matches expected.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: `qwen3.6-plus` live on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` present (flag corrected to `true`).
- **Edge cases checked**: None beyond CN Coding Plan endpoint.
- **What I did NOT verify**: Global Coding Plan endpoint (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) — not maintained by this contributor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — removes a suppression; Coding Plan CN users who previously could not select `qwen3.6-plus` can now do so. No existing working configuration is broken.
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: `qwen3.6-plus` may not be available on all Coding Plan CN accounts (account-level activation required).
  - Mitigation: Users without access will receive a vendor-side model-not-found error, same as any unavailable model; no crash or data loss.
- **Risk**: Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior unknown — suppression removed for that endpoint too as a side effect.
  - Mitigation: If Global Coding Plan does not support `qwen3.6-plus`, affected users will see a vendor error. A follow-up can re-add a suppression scoped to `coding-intl` only if confirmed unavailable there.
